name: recall-vscode-extension
description: Personal, local-first developer memory — silent capture, in-context resurfacing, standups, and MCP for AI agents.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Recall quietly captures your file saves, terminal commands, git activity, and more, then helps you find it again later.

Nothing here yet? Try asking it something, or save your first note manually.

[Ask Recall a question](command:recall.askRecall)
[Save a note as memory](command:recall.saveAsMemory)

Everything stays on your machine — see the [Welcome walkthrough](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22recall.recall-vscode-extension%23recall.welcome%22) for what's captured and how to pause it.

walkthroughs.title: Welcome to Recall
walkthroughs.description: Recall quietly captures your debugging process and helps you find it again later — entirely on your machine, encrypted, and pausable any time.

steps.title: What Recall captures
steps.description: File saves, terminal commands, git activity, debug sessions, and diagnostics — turned into a private, searchable memory. File edits are captured as **diffs**, never the full file content.

steps.title: Where it's stored
steps.description: Everything lives locally under `~/.recall/` — nothing leaves your machine unless you explicitly opt in to sync later.

steps.title: Pause anytime, redact automatically
steps.description:
Click the Recall item in the status bar to pause capture instantly. A redaction pipeline strips likely secrets before anything is saved — try it yourself.
[Test Redaction](command:recall.testRedaction)

steps.title: Try it now
steps.description:
Ask Recall something about your own past work — it answers from what it's already captured, with citations back to the source.
[Ask Recall a question](command:recall.askRecall)

The same button lives in the Recall sidebar's "..." menu any time, alongside Daily Standup and Weekly Summary.
